title: Smart URL caps in --table: don't truncate URLs, truncate desc first
description:
docs/specs/drafts/output-readability.md §"Option A".

Today --table caps homepage at 40 and description at 50, so URLs
get …-truncated and become unclickable. User has to re-query.

Fix: column-budget allocation that gives URL columns priority.
Concretely:
- Detect URL-shaped columns (starts with http(s)://).
- If terminal width permits, never cap them.
- Truncate description first when budget is tight.
- New flag --urls-full: emit URLs on a trailing line per row.

Pairs with format-color.md (color in --table) — both touch the
table renderer; land together or sequentially.

Driver: output-readability.md, user note 2026-05-28 "it sucks to
cutoff urls because I can't click them."

title: Filter 'Bump to' commits from generated tag-message body
Vocabulary spec: unify source/kind naming across -C, timing, indicators
description:
The current scheme is source-first (`installed:f`, `local:c`, `taps`).
User flagged "keep forgetting which order" as a real ergonomic problem.

Several places need to agree on one spelling:
- `-C` cache-status rows (current: `installed:f`)
- Timing footer labels (draft spec: `installed:f`, `refresh:installed:f`)
- Installed-indicator column (draft spec just uses ●/○)
- `--refresh=KIND` selector (current: `installed`, short `i`)
- Table column headers (current: `S` = single letter)

Candidates:
A. Keep source-first long form everywhere: `installed:formula`,
   `local:cask`, `taps`. Most readable, longest.
B. Compact two-letter (`if`, `lc`, `t`) — short but cryptic.
C. Type-first reversal: `f:installed`, `c:local` — reads "formula
   from installed", aligns with how -f/-c are kind filters today.
D. Single canonical long, single canonical short, both documented:
   `installed:formula` ↔ `i:f`; aliases freely.

Write a spec doc at docs/specs/drafts/cli-vocabulary.md.

Driver: timing.md "Open questions" + installed-indicator.md cross-refs.

Old flag forms keep working as sticky aliases dispatching to subcommands. Shared global adverbs: --quiet --verbose --offline --stale --refresh --color --no-timing format flags. Per-subcommand --help screens replace the flat surface. Blocked by cli-vocabulary Option A bead (bhs-qms).

title: BUG: -H history shows index version, should show installed version
close_reason: Fixed in 650ecc5; recorded installed[].version per keg, casks prefer installed field. Regression tests in tests/test_history.py

title: Universalize --stale across all sources (formula, installed, taps, local)
description: See docs/specs/drafts/cli-vocabulary.md §Problem 2. Today --stale DUR is only threaded into api.ensure_cache (formula/cask). Installed/taps/local sources have hardcoded stale thresholds that --stale doesn't touch. Plan: bare --stale DUR applies to every source this invocation touches; selector form --stale=KIND:DUR[,KIND:DUR] mirrors --refresh=KIND. Coordinate with cli-vocabulary Option A. Driver: user note 2026-05-28 -- support --stale and --refresh on all the options.

title: Interactive viewer: bhs -I → less -S shim (zero new deps)
description: See docs/specs/drafts/output-readability.md §Variant 1. When stdout is a TTY, bhs -I python pipes default output through less -S. User side-scrolls wide rows; no row truncation. Future fzf-style picker (Variant 2) and bespoke TUI (Variant 3) are separate beads. -I letter choice (collides with -i installed) — cli-vocabulary proposes uppercase -I under verbs-and-special-modes rule.

title: OSC 8 hyperlinks in default output (clickable names on modern terminals)
description:
docs/specs/drafts/output-readability.md §"Option B" — URL clickable
without a dedicated column.

Wrap name in OSC 8: \033]8;;URL\007name\033]8;;\007. Modern
terminals (iTerm.app, WezTerm, Kitty, Alacritty, vscode) render
the name as a clickable link. Zero width cost.

Detection allowlist (initial):
- TERM_PROGRAM in {iTerm.app, WezTerm, Kitty, vscode}
- TERM startsWith alacritty
- Conservative; degrades to plain on non-matching terminals.

Opt-in env: BREW_HOP_SEARCH_FORCE_OSC8=1.
Opt-out env: BREW_HOP_SEARCH_NO_OSC8=1.

tmux: by default OSC 8 doesn't pass through; flag for review at
implementation, may need passthrough config note in docs.

Driver: output-readability.md.

title: Scoped verbosity sub-args: -v=t, -v=tt, -v=ttcl style
description:
User proposal: instead of a single linear -v/-vv/-vvv ladder,
accept letter-tagged increments where each character bumps one
dimension's verbosity.

Examples:
  -v=t       # +1 timing detail
  -v=tt      # +2 timing detail
  -v=ttcl    # +2 timing, +1 cache, +1 labels
  -v=c       # default everything else, just more cache detail

Bare -v / -vv keeps current behavior (linear ladder); the kv form
is for users who want to dial in one axis without dragging the
others up.

Dimensions to consider:
- t : timing footer (default 1 line; tt = per-source; ttt = per-step)
- c : cache section (default minimal; c = per-table; cc = with TTL/ETA)
- l : labels (default short kind:f; l = installed:formula full)
- o : op/operation trace (debug-tier, exposes the timer context)

Risk: argparse doesn't natively support this; needs a custom action.
Risk: collides with --verbose=N alias.

Driver: timing.md "Open questions" §3.

Defer pending: vocabulary spec landing (the letter set has to map
to canonical dimension names).

title: Add --offline adverb; rename -L → -l (cli-vocabulary Option A)
description: See docs/specs/drafts/cli-vocabulary.md §Option A. (1) Add --offline (no short letter): forbids network refresh for this invocation, regardless of source; mutually exclusive with --refresh. (2) Lowercase -L → -l (local source is a noun alongside -i and -t; lowercase matches the rule). Keep -L as a hidden alias for one release with -v deprecation hint. (3) Update cache-flow.md and OUTPUT.md. Driver: user 2026-05-28: -L confusion (noun or adverb?). Pairs with --stale universalization.

title: Stale-result hash for diff detection in cache-flow trailing line
description:
docs/specs/features/cache-flow.md promises a trailing line variant
"↻ updated, results may differ — re-run". Realizing this requires
the foreground process to remember what it printed, then re-run the
same FTS query against the post-refresh cache and diff.

Two implementations on the table:
A. Snapshot the printed `(name, version)` tuples in memory; rerun
   query, compare set equality.
B. Hash the result envelope (e.g. sha256 over a canonical JSON
   serialization). Cheaper memory, harder to debug.

Either way the data has to live until the bg refresh's sentinel
fires (which is bounded by the cache-flow grace window: 2s default).

Open: do we store this between invocations (so a second `bhs python`
N seconds later can show the diff against the previously-printed
result set)? Probably no — scope creep.

Driver: cache-flow.md "Differs/matches detection" section + the
2026-05-28 grace-window change.

title: Long-horizon: install-manager / multi-select TUI (aptitude-shaped)
description: See docs/specs/drafts/output-readability.md §Variant 2/3. Stay-mode picker with preview pane, multi-select, selected-for-install queue surviving across commands. fzf-style narrowing on visible result set; description-shaped filter while package list scrolls. Crosses from search tool to install manager. Prereqs: cli-vocabulary subcommand surface (bhs install?), selection state model, UX paradigm choice. Marker bead so it doesn't get smuggled into a smaller scope.
